name: Cloudflare HTTP Method Enforcement Pack
objective: Reduce attack surface by blocking or challenging uncommon/high-risk HTTP methods and tightly scoping state-changing methods to approved API paths.
risk_mitigated: HTTP verb tampering and method confusion attacks
Reconnaissance and proxy abuse via TRACE/CONNECT/TRACK
Unauthorized data modification attempts using PUT/DELETE/PATCH outside intended API routes
description: This pack enforces a conservative HTTP method policy for internet-facing applications. It immediately controls high-risk legacy/tunneling verbs (TRACE, CONNECT, TRACK) and provides an optional rule to restrict PUT/DELETE/PATCH to explicit API prefixes.

rules.description: Block high-risk/uncommon HTTP methods TRACE, CONNECT, and TRACK
Optional: Restrict PUT/DELETE/PATCH to approved API paths
rules.expression: http.request.method in {"PUT" "DELETE" "PATCH"} and not starts_with(http.request.uri.path, "/api/") and not starts_with(http.request.uri.path, "/v1/")

tuning_notes: Inventory valid methods per application and endpoint before enabling blocking actions.
If WebDAV or proxy/tunnel behavior is intentionally used, explicitly exempt trusted paths/hosts before enabling the risky-verb block.
Adjust allowed write-method path prefixes (for example /api/, /v1/, /internal-api/) to match your API routing design.
For browser/API clients that cannot complete challenges reliably, use log mode first and then move directly to block after validation.
deployment_notes: Safe rollout: deploy both rules in non-blocking posture first (log/challenge).
Phase 1 (48-72h): set risky-verb rule to managed_challenge or log-equivalent and optional write-verb scope rule to managed_challenge; review events.
Phase 2: promote risky-verb rule to block after confirming no legitimate traffic uses TRACE/CONNECT/TRACK.
Phase 3: enable and enforce write-verb path restriction (managed_challenge -> block) after API owner sign-off and exception handling.
monitoring_guidance: Track request counts by method over time; alert on sudden spikes in TRACE/CONNECT/TRACK attempts.
Monitor managed challenge solve rate and abandonment for PUT/DELETE/PATCH requests.
Review top blocked/challenged paths and source ASNs/IPs to identify scanners and misconfigured clients.
Create a short-lived rollout dashboard segmented by rule ID to catch false positives early.
false_positive_considerations: Legacy integrations, diagnostics tools, or non-browser clients may use uncommon methods unexpectedly.
Admin tools and CI/CD jobs may issue PUT/DELETE/PATCH outside standard public API prefixes.
Multi-tenant apps with custom path structures should tune prefix conditions carefully before enforcement.
